channel.title: The Grant Desk - Research & Journalism, Remote
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: Research & Journalism, Remote.

item.title: Paid Research Assistant: AI and the War in Ukraine
item.description:
Organisation: Bogna Konior (NYU Shanghai)

Location: Remote-friendly.

Award: USD 18 per hour; 10-15 hours per week, flexible schedule.

Duration: Ongoing part-time research assistantship; start as soon as possible.

Deadline: 23 Jul 2026

Bogna Konior, Assistant Professor of Media Theory at NYU Shanghai, is hiring a paid research assistant for a project on Ukraine and artificial intelligence. The work supports a forthcoming book with MIT Press, plus an independent scenario she is writing on the future of AI with the war as a lens. WHAT THE WORK LOOKS LIKE: going month-by-month through news archives (New York Times, Reuters, Financial Times, and specialist outlets), annotating and cataloguing coverage of AI, drones, and autonomous systems in the war, plus other developments she will direct you to; building and maintaining a structured Notion database covering chronology, companies, industries, and key actors; and organizing and editing her existing notes. WHO SHE IS LOOKING FOR: interest in and general knowledge of AI; prior research and annotation experience, careful and detail-oriented, no sloppy citations; comfortable with Notion and with AI tools; Ukrainian language skill a strong plus (not required); familiarity with AI, war, tech, and geopolitics news sources is great. Remote-friendly, start ASAP. She will also consider people with no prior experience, as well as current and previous students. TERMS: USD 18 per hour, 10-15 hours per week, flexible schedule. TO APPLY: email bogna@nyu.edu with a short introduction and background (link to website, socials, CV, LinkedIn, or portfolio if you have one); where you are based and your weekly availability; and one recent AI-related story, company, or development you find interesting and why (a vibe check). Bonus: a sample of past research or writing if you have it.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: RYSE Innovation Hub: Youth Innovation Advisor
item.description:
Organisation: RYSE Innovation Hub

Location: 100% remote.

Award: USD 480 monthly compensation. No application fee.

Duration: September 2026 to July 2027; up to 4 hours per week; 100% remote.

Deadline: 20 Jul 2026

RYSE Innovation Hub seeks Youth Innovation Advisors: young people ages 18 to 24 who are passionate about adolescent health and wellness. Youth Innovation Advisors support projects that strengthen youth engagement practices and help shape health solutions created with young people in mind. The role is 100% remote, running from September 2026 to July 2027, with a time commitment of up to 4 hours per week. WHO IT'S FOR: young people ages 18 to 24 passionate about adolescent health and wellness. COMPENSATION: USD 480 monthly. DEADLINE: 20 July 2026, 11:59 PM ET. TO APPLY: submit the application form.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Digital Impact Alliance (DIAL) Research Fellowship
item.description:
Organisation: Digital Impact Alliance (DIAL)

Location: Remote; international organisation. Experience working in or with global majority countries encouraged.

Award: USD 5,000 stipend for a 6-8 month fellowship. No application fee.

Duration: 6-8 month research fellowship; rolling applications

Deadline: Rolling / undated

The Digital Impact Alliance (DIAL) Research Fellowship supports curious, passionate mid-career researchers and practitioners to explore frontier questions on digital public infrastructure (DPI) and data governance. Fellows bring their own knowledge and research agenda while contributing to DIAL's core thematic priorities: creating more value for more people from data; understanding how digital systems and services impact people, including and especially the most marginalised; and accelerating the adoption of data-sharing and governance practices that fuel innovation and empower people. SUPPORT: a USD 5,000 stipend for research completed over 6-8 months, plus mentorship from DIAL staff and industry experts, access to DIAL's network and resources, collaboration with partner organisations, and support amplifying the work. Each fellow produces two preliminary pieces and one final, more extensive output (research papers or reports, policy briefs, expert-comment series, etc.). ELIGIBILITY: professionals from academia, the private sector, and civil society, typically with 8-15 years of experience in digital development, policy, or research; demonstrated work at the intersection of technology and society (including DPI and data governance); a track record of high-quality writing, research, or policy briefs; experience working in or with global majority countries; and strong analytical and communication skills. TO APPLY (rolling basis): submit a CV or resume, a writing sample or portfolio, and a research proposal. Questions: fellows@dial.global. Apply via https://dial.global/apply-to-our-fellows-program/.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: David McClure Public Interest Journalism Fellowship 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Oxford International Centre for Publishing, Oxford Brookes University

Location: Fully remote

Award: GBP 15,000 grant to support one nonfiction writing fellow. No application fee.

Duration: Fellowship starts each September (funding for one fellow per year for five years)

Deadline: Closed 31 May 2026

Fellowship from the Oxford International Centre for Publishing at Oxford Brookes University, supporting one nonfiction writer per year (for the next five years) with a GBP 15,000 award to work on a timely, independent piece of long-form public interest journalism that might otherwise never be published. The investigation must be original, non-biased, non-partisan and rigorously researched, with the decision based on how deserving the work is to be in the public domain. Named for David McClure, the news producer, author and journalist known for his forensic investigations into institutional privilege and the British Royal family's finances. APPLICATION: a 1,500-word pitch including a synopsis of the investigation and its aims, objectives, key research undertaken and progress to date, the support needed to complete it, and how the funds will be used. The fellow starts in September; the annual application deadline is 31 May. Fully remote.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: BBQ+ Academic Fellowship 2026-27
item.description:
Organisation: Center for Black, Brown, and Queer Studies (BBQ+), supported by the Mellon Foundation

Location: Remote (fully virtual organisation; all fellowship meetings via Zoom with closed captioning)

Award: Tiered research stipend by academic level: College students $1,000; Master's/Early PhD $2,000; Advanced PhD $5,000; Recent postdoctoral scholars $10,000. Fellows may also apply to reimburse other research or professionalisation expenses.

Duration: Approximately mid-September 2026 through end of April 2027 (~8 months); weekly Friday meetings 12:00 to 14:00 ET

Deadline: Closed 01 May 2026

Annual academic fellowship for underrepresented scholars (broadly and intersectionally defined) working in critical race, Indigenous, postcolonial and queer studies. Open to college students (Sophomores and up, including community college students), recent graduates, Master's students and recent Master's holders (up to 3 years post-degree), PhD/EdD/other doctoral students, and recent postdoctoral scholars (up to 8 years post-degree). Parental, family, sick and similar leaves do not count toward years-post-degree. Open to undocumented and international applicants. Fellows are placed in one of four cohorts (Undergraduate, Master's & Early Doctoral, Advanced Doctoral/Dissertation, Postdoctoral) with a dedicated cohort advisor. Weekly meetings alternate among Writing Clinic, Theory Clinic, Colloquium and Fellows Circle. Application materials: 1-page cover letter, 1-2 page research proposal, CV, and academic writing sample of at least 8 pages of continuous prose. Deadline 1 May 2026 by midnight ET; review begins 4 May 2026; late applications unlikely to receive full consideration. Questions: fellowships@bbqplus.org.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).
